description_long: This is a rectangular, two-story brick factory building with a flat, light gray roof and a series of large, evenly spaced industrial windows along its length. The facade features a repetitive grid of windows, each with white framing, and the brickwork is a classic red with visible mortar lines. At one end, there is a stairway leading up to a door, and the building is flanked by a concrete loading dock with ramps and barriers for vehicle access. Several street lamps and utility poles are positioned around the perimeter, and there are visible safety railings and bollards near the loading areas. The ground surface is a mix of concrete and asphalt, with some painted lines and a fenced area nearby. No smokestacks or rooftop machinery are visible, and the overall appearance is utilitarian, typical of mid-20th-century industrial architecture.
description: A rectangular red-brick factory building with large industrial windows, a flat gray roof, and a concrete loading dock with ramps and safety railings.
description_view_0: End view showing the stairway, side brick wall, and the length of the loading dock with ramps and barriers.
description_view_1: Long side view displaying the full row of large industrial windows, the main brick facade, and the loading dock area.
description_view_2: Opposite end view highlighting the other side of the building, the loading dock, and the adjacent fenced area.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back brick wall with windows and the flat roof, with minimal loading dock features visible.
